entries.tipText: Pick up the supply crate beside Alien7's stall.
Constant Companion is close - a straight burn will do. Watch your landing speed near the base.
The base is unlocked. Nobody locks a door twelve years after the last visitor.
Land on the trailing terminator - the line between day and night. The dayside will cook your ship's systems.
Ship power drains fast near the light. Park in shadow, walk the rest.
Three beacons. The camp is orderly. Try not to think about how orderly.
Someone who still asks questions. The concerts attract people who don't like being listened to.
Tumbling Bean has no stable 'down'. Land slow, and register everything you value with your grip.
The wrecks cluster where the tumble is gentlest. Parts sell at the Ship Market.
Cyclops is far. Let a close pass at a heavy body bend your path - speed is free if you ask gravity politely.
The platform floats in the upper haze. Your solar panel is useless in Cyclops's shadow - budget power for the descent.
Aim the instrument at the Pupil. Try not to feel aimed at in return.
The north stage only runs on the night side. Wait for the planet to turn, or fly ahead of the dark.
Take the stage with the guitar. Ninety seconds. Play like you mean it.
Alien3 keeps every tape he's ever traded for. He'll want something rare - or something played.
The ship's cassette deck still works. Side A first.
The fishing hole is inside the outpost, where the vent water runs warm under the ice.
They want you to eat it here. Raw. They wrote 'we are not proud of it' and underlined it twice.
The stranded ship is inside the shadow cone - no sun, no charge, no radio. You'll be the only light out there.
Hold your artificial sun over their solar panel and wait. Rescue is mostly patience.
Watchful Eye is the outermost body. Slingshot past Cyclops - HAL will chart the window if you ask.
The infrastructure is older than anyone alive. Land anywhere. It has been expecting traffic for sixty years.
